{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PEX tubing passing through concrete slab without protective sleeve",
    "Direct contact between PEX and concrete, risking abrasion and chemical degradation",
    "No allowance for thermal expansion/contraction at slab penetration"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Expose PEX at slab penetration and install approved protective sleeve or conduit",
    "Verify sleeve extends above slab and is sealed per manufacturer instructions",
    "Replace any PEX section showing signs of abrasion or damage"
  ],
  "rationale": "PEX must be sleeved when passing through concrete slabs to protect against abrasion, chemical attack, and to allow for movement. IPC and manufacturer instructions require isolation from direct concrete contact.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 305.4 (Piping through foundation walls)",
    "PEX manufacturer installation guidelines (e.g., Uponor, Viega) requiring sleeving at slab penetrations"
  ]
}
